claim: Re-ranking solves LLM overload: pull large chunk count from vector DB, use cross-encoder to filter to top N before passing to LLM
actionable: Implement re-ranking in RAG pipelines; use specialized models to reduce context token count while maintaining coverage
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Agentic RAG lets agents choose search mode: semantic search, full document read, or hybrid based on question
actionable: Give RAG agents tool choices for search strategy; provide both vector search and document-level access
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Knowledge graphs store entity relationships; slow/expensive to build with LLMs but fantastic for interconnected data
actionable: Use knowledge graphs for highly relational domains (legal, financial, medical); accept extraction cost
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Contextual retrieval prepends chunk context at indexing time using LLM; slower/expensive but improves retrieval quality
actionable: Use contextual retrieval for ambiguous domains; accept upfront LLM cost for better retrieval precision
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Query expansion and multi-query RAG both expand search coverage; multi-query uses parallel queries for better coverage
actionable: Use multi-query RAG for complex information needs; accept extra LLM calls for better search coverage
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Context-aware chunking preserves document structure; semantic boundaries better than fixed character/token splits
actionable: Use embedding models to find natural chunk boundaries; tools like Dockling do hybrid chunking automatically
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Hierarchical RAG balances precision and context: search small for precision, return parent document for full context
actionable: Use hierarchical RAG for large documents; store parent-child chunk relationships in metadata
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Self-reflective RAG grades retrieval quality 1-5 and retries with refined search if below threshold
actionable: Implement self-correcting RAG loops with grading thresholds to improve retrieval quality at cost of extra LLM calls
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Fine-tuning embeddings on domain data yields 5-10% accuracy gains; enables sentiment-based similarity vs semantic similarity
actionable: Fine-tune embeddings for domain-specific tasks (sentiment, legal terms); requires domain training dataset
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)

claim: Optimal solution combines 3-5 RAG strategies: recommend re-ranking, agentic RAG, and context-aware chunking as baseline
actionable: Start with re-ranking, agentic RAG, and hybrid chunking; layer additional strategies based on domain needs
source_title: Every RAG Strategy Explained in 13 Minutes (No Fluff)
